mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 9.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 9.5% or remains above 9.0% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite elevated UNRATE, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales combined with falling housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, reducing near-term corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 600 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367670M and HOUST at 598k suggest demand softness
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS and HOUST declines falls below 0.30 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining below 93.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate profit margins and equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 92.5 or declines > 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day returns following INDPRO declines are positive in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with stable inflation signals healthy labor demand and wage growth capacity without cost-push pressures, supporting corporate earnings and producing positive 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 0.2% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.5%
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 expanding above trend signals strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13194.9B indicates baseline demand level
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant difference between periods of above-trend vs. below-trend GDPC1 growth (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.0% annually combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward valuations, producing negative 60-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS explains < 5% of variance in forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining while unemployment rises simultaneously signals stagflationary pressure where supply constraints meet weakening demand, producing negative 20-day equity returns as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.21 and UNRATE at 9.6% suggest mixed pressures
regime_hint: stagflationary environment or supply shock with labor weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.
